summary: This class has reading, writing, listening, and speaking components. We'll be looking at many themes connected with life in the 21st century.
aims:
This course will emphasize all four skills of listening, speaking, reading, and writing, though more emphasis will be put on listening and writing skills. Writing activities will include a self-introduction, an interview, book and film reviews and opinion pieces. There will be an emphasis on individual work, pair work, and group work. Students will also practice free writing and writing of book reports to be turned in regularly. There will be a speed reading component to the class.
Communicative activities relating to some of the writing assignments will also form a part of the class.
goals: to learn strategies for writing in English
to practice practical types of writing such as personal profiles, narratives, cause/effect and reviews
to gain functional language through content based activities
to learn how to express oneself logically in English

plan.text: Languages Around the World/Introduction of Graded Readers

textbooks: In Focus 1 Academic by Charles Browne,Brent Culligan And Joseph Phillips
prereq: Students should have some basic familiarity with the English language before taking the class.
prep: Students should review textbook assignments at home. There will be writing assignments where students will be required to rewrite their papers following editing guidelines.

extra.text:
The following factors will be taken into account in determining student scores.
1. Class attendance. Students must attend at least 2/3 of the classes in each term to receive a passing
grade. Two late attendances equal one absence. Students over 30 minutes late will be considered absent.
Grading Percentages
1. Timed writing assignments. (10%)
2. Book Reports/Speed Reading (10%)
3. Active participation in communicative activities (10%)
4. Online assignments (20%)
5. Examinations/End of Term Essays (50%).
Students must have a score of at least 60 to pass the course
